text.title: Rust HTTP handler rules: no &str, no unwrap, cargo check loop
text.summary: Concrete rules for Axum handler authoring that prevent the most common compiler errors and runtime panics produced by LLM-generated code.

rule.statement: All handler function parameters must be owned types (String, not &str). Lifetime parameters do not satisfy Axum's Handler trait bound.
rule.rationale: Axum handlers must be Send + 'static. References with non-static lifetimes break this invariant. Agents trained on sync examples emit &str; always replace with String.

rule.statement: Never use .unwrap() or .expect() in handler code paths. Use ? to propagate into AppError. Block unwrap_used via clippy: -D clippy::unwrap_used.
rule.rationale: unwrap() panics on None/Err, which terminates the current task and may leak resources. Axum catches panics but the response is a 500 with no body context.

rule.statement: Use cargo check as the primary feedback loop during handler development (10x faster than cargo build). Run cargo build + cargo test only before commit.
rule.rationale: cargo check type-checks without linking. For Axum with its generic extractor machinery, full builds are expensive. cargo check catches 95% of errors in <5s.

rule.statement: Test handlers with Router::oneshot(Request::builder()...) — no real server needed. This approach is fast, parallel-safe, and tests the full extractor + middleware chain.
rule.rationale: Spinning a real server for tests adds port-conflict flakiness and startup overhead. oneshot runs the full handler pipeline in-process.

antipattern.description: Adding 'a lifetime parameters to handler signatures to "fix" &str params.
antipattern.reason: Lifetime params do not satisfy Axum's Into<Router> bound. The correct fix is to use String (owned) or 'static &str constants.

antipattern.description: Placing sqlx::query! without DATABASE_URL or .sqlx/ offline files in CI.
antipattern.reason: sqlx queries are verified at compile time; CI builds without DATABASE_URL set will fail. Use SQLX_OFFLINE=true and check in .sqlx/.
Holding a std::sync::MutexGuard across .await deadlocks the Tokio runtime. Use tokio::sync::Mutex or prefer message-passing.
